title: log::warn\! in key Drop impls instead of panic or silent no-op
description: All five HSM key types (RsaKey, MlDsaKey, EccP256Key, Ed25519Key, Curve25519Key) emit log::warn\! when dropped without prior eviction, naming the leaked key ID and pointing to the safe alternative (with_*_key() or .evict()). A future contributor might suggest removing the Drop impl entirely ('the user should just remember to call evict') or replacing it with a panic. Neither is correct: silently dropping leaks the HSM cache slot with zero feedback in production; panicking is wrong in Drop because it causes an abort if a second panic is already unwinding. log::warn\! is the right severity — it is visible in logs, filterable, and never fatal. The key ID in the message is essential for post-mortem tracing. Do not weaken this to a no-op or remove the id field from the message.
